reason: Adding an optional second global hotkey that opens the wheel at the orb's home position instead of at the cursor is a functional/settings addition, not a redesign. HotkeyService gains an id parameter so a second shortcut registers under its own id alongside the primary one; OverlayWindow registers/clears it at startup and on save and disposes it on close. The UI diff only adds one text field plus a live status line to the existing hotkey block of the settings window, reusing the same validation and status colours as the primary hotkey field. The wheel, the orb, the toast look and layout are untouched, and no new colour or visual language was introduced, so there is nothing for a mockup set to compare against.
